title: clematopsis uhehensis
side_effects: Clematopsis uhehensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues due to its potent volatile oils. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap may cause burns, blisters, and skin lesions, while ingesting even small amounts can lead to nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In severe cases, improper use of Clematopsis uhehensis can result in kidney damage, seizures, and even coma. Pregnant women and individuals with pre-existing medical conditions should exercise extreme caution when handling or using the plant, as it can exacerbate underlying health issues.
